seoTitle: Edge Hill vs Edmonton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Edge Hill and Edmonton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Edge Hill vs Edmonton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Edge Hill and Edmonton.
heading: Edge Hill vs Edmonton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Edge Hill, Queensland and Edmonton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Edge Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Edmonton (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Edge Hill or Edmonton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Edge Hill has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Edmonton scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Edge Hill scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Edmonton scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Edge Hill or Edmonton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Edge Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
